channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: We need to talk. And I mean a serious conversation. What if that was the bottom of the bear market? With all the blatant… — 34 replies — 86.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
We need to talk. And I mean a serious conversation. What if that was the bottom of the bear market? With all the blatant…

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-04-08 12:13 UTC

OP

We need to talk. And I mean a serious conversation.

What if that was the bottom of the bear market? With all the blatant manipulation, the threats, and the current mess, it feels like BTC just doesn’t want to go below 65k

I'm a perp trader and I'm curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62090678
>>62090672
that would suck because i closed my long at 69999

62090679
>>62090672
qrd on the ho?

62090689
>>62090678
Most perp traders and crypto quants expect to go below the 200 week MA, that would put us around 50-55k. But I myself am very surprised at BTC's resilience.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62090704
>>62090696
like a female satto?

62090726
>what if it takes extreme geopolitical events include threats of nuclear war to scam a 2% pump?
It's so fucking over

62090734
Anybody else in disbelief that we’ve been in a bear market for quite some time now? I’m over here thinking alt season hasn’t even begun so clearly the bull market hasn’t started. But I guess it ended some time ago. Like I didn’t even make money this time. Time is going by so fast, Jesus I’m getting old

62090743
>>62090734
Bro, it was pretty obvious after what happened in october. There could be some doubts when we were hanging around 90k, but after that...

Good news for you. For now, the 4 year cycle paradigm is still on. Even if we haven't hit rock bottom, the worst of the bear market is behind us. A few more months, brother

62090763
180 iq here. that was not the bottom. There was too much excitement with the war keeping people glued to the charts. the market makers paint the charts to create boring price action so people get so frustrated that it's not doing anything they get bored and stop paying attention. That's when you'll get the real bottom

62090769
>>62090743
I genuinely think crypto is dead. Everybody would rather play options or bet in prediction markets. My mistake was holding onto LINK really believing I’d become filthy rich. It’s now dawning on me I’ve been holding for 8 years with so little to show for it it’s embarrassing. I could’ve been holding literally anything besides crypto and I probably would be replying to you from my dream home right now. It’s over

62090826
>>62090769
it's not dead, but a lot of shitcoins are going to die (like LINK). BTC and probably ETH along with stablecoins are going to survive. You got suckered into a cult

62090845
>>62090826
Similarly wasting $100 every month on BTC and ETH won’t bring you your youth back. It’s over big dawg

62090849
>>62090769
Crypto is a psyop. It's an IQ test. Unfortunately the majority of money has already been extracted and idiots are almost completely out of ammo it makes no sense to pump it any more to give them an exit.

Everyone who took profit took that money and bought things like gold, stocks, houses, cars and such. Nobody with a brain actually believes in crypto because yielding assets in tradfi are better since you don't need to sell them to get money from them most of the time, and well, they represent something real, like a company selling actual things people need, and making money from that. Crypto is just money passing hands, like musical chairs.

62090881
>>62090763
Can I suck your cock?

62090939
>>62090672
>That entire crashout was over less than $4k
This is why women and other emotionally unstable people should never engage in active trading I've been down 70k in one day which was nearly 1/5th of my networth at the time and I sure as hell didn't throw a tantrum and panic sell.

62090942
>>62090939
>I've been down 70k in one day which was nearly 1/5th of my networth at the time
What was your trade and how much leverage did you use? How did that trade end?

62090954
>>62090939
They're a streamer, and they're acting. The real money comes from engagement and simps.

62091190
>>62090849
Same as most currencies, the only thing that gives them value is belief.

The only reason they pay interest is collective belief and for the same reason you can get interest from deposited btc.

The problem is most currencies in the world are being cucked due to Jewish tricks. That's why btc was invented as a standard unit of value, digital gold.

The idea that it doesn't produce anything therefore it's useless applies to income generating assets, not fundamental core belief systems like USD or BTC.

USD is protected by increasingly limp wristed men with guns and deranged presidents.

BTC and Eth are protected by cryptography and the internet itself.

62091396
>>62090672
tfw no mentally ill gambling gf

62091441
>>62090734
i am in disbelief how everything is green and all my investments are greener than ever or at ath
according to the experts at /biz/ we are in a bear market btw

62091459
Anons find me a jav pornstar who looks similar pls

62091559
>>62091190
I know. I am cash poor for good reason, and I don't hold any crypto because it is equally as shit.

Equities, on the other hand I hold a lot of.

62091561
>>62090672

62091562
>>62091190
Also bitcoin is not digital gold because it can't be used for anything except being a non government issued fiat.

Gold has physical uses and demand as jewellery so it has floor to the price from marginal buyers.

That's why the value of bitcoin is so volatile, is because the price is purely speculative and there is no marginal buyer except for cuckold saylor.

62091571
>>62090734
>disbelief
>quite some time
>getting old
something doesn't add up here

62091593
>>62090679
>the ho
It's Miss Jadoodoo for you, peasant.

62091602
the manipulation is the new normal, lmao, better get used to it

62091605
>>62090672
I told you in February that it was the bottom

62091639
>>62090769
If there's one hard lesson that my stupid ass finally learned after nearly a decade of trying and failing to make it in crypto, it's this:
Other than Bitcoin (maybe ETH too), anyone who bought into and held a crypto that then 10x'd minimum their initial investment and didn't sell and is now too far back down in value for a meaningful profit, is a fucking idiot
Now of course there are cases where lucky idiots hold on to something for longer, and get a 20x-50x or more before selling for profit. Lucky them, that shit rarely happens and it's very risky
But still....never. Marry. Your. Bags. Buy into one, ride the wave of new speculators to a 5x (10x maximum), sell and move the fuck on to the next one for another possible 5x-10x
I don't care if the original bag then goes on to 1000x after you sell, motherfucker you still 10x'd your money. YOU STILL WON. STOP BEING FUCKING GREEDY

62091648
>>62090769
i keep telling you retards that polymarkets/kalshi is the new shitcoin but you don't want to listen, keep shilling your shitcoins while everyone moves on, if you can't see how big polymarkets kind of market could become where you will be able to literally bet on anything, you can't be helped, the world is honestly changing faster than we would normally predict and few people have a clue

62091649
>>62091602
>new
lmao

62091656
>>62091649
new as in institutions admitting it or not bothering to hide it

62091780
>>62091396
>its OK babe
>here's your kneepads and hair tie; I'll be in my favorite chair

62091846
>>62091639
>stop being greedy

lmao...no

item.title: NUCLEAR HAPPENING — 20 replies — 80.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
NUCLEAR HAPPENING

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-04-08 12:36 UTC

OP

BIGGEST CRYPTO HAPPENING EVER

Early replies (first 3)

62091631
>>62091626
>shit nobody cares about

62091642
>>62091626
Proof?

62091647
I am actually Satoshi. I can say this because no one will ever believe it. The man in OP is not me.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62091654
>>62091626
This is like the tenth time he's been accused of being Satoshi. How come he hasn't been kidnapped? They kidnap bitcoin millionaires all the time. This guy is a trillionaire.

62091663
>>62091626
kidnapping imminent

62091665
they didn't find a satoshi for like 5 years now. i was beginning to worry

62091667
Adam Back has been speculated to be Satoshi almost as long as Bitcoin has been around. nothingburger

62091668
>>62091654
Presumably he would have sold already if he had the keys.

62091674
>>62091626
isnt that pretty much getting asked to get abducted with your name and face right next to satoshi nakamoto.

62091675
>Some br*tish incel made my tokens
Ew bearish sell sell sell

62091693
>>62091626
It's so obvious this pic related faggot is Satoshi.
>Nakamoto ... turned libertarian
>when Goodman asked him about bitcoin during a brief in-person interview, Nakamoto seemed to confirm his identity as its founder, saying: "I am no longer involved in that and I cannot discuss it. It's been turned over to other people. They are in charge of it now. I no longer have any connection."
He obviously had shit-tier opsec (using his birth name and present surname as a pseudonym) and zero ability to lie, and his glowie handlers weren't prepared. Soon, the handlers take over and:
>Later that day, the pseudonymous Nakamoto's P2P Foundation account posted its first message in five years: "I am not Dorian Nakamoto."
Why would the real Satoshi even care? He said nothing when other people were accused of being Satoshi. This message is 100% proof that he is Satoshi.
The handlers also make sure the press focuses on all other Bitcoin early contributors when looking for his identity and keep ignoring the real one.

62091713
>>62091626
yes, we knew

62091810
>When everyone finds out that some faggot named Adam Back is Satoshi, BITCOIN WILL GO TO ZERO!!!

62091817
Nice try fed but Satoshi Nakamoto translates to central intelligence

62091822
>advertising blog for boomers has an ad
Okay. Nobody under 50 cares
gtfo

62091827
>>62091817
This is fucking retarded, you don’t understand Japanese at all.
Sometimes I forget how retarded the other anons on this site really are.

62091848
>>62091693
This.
I think Back keeps getting accused of being Satoshi because he's the one that happens to be in al the right places when happenings start and probably has a significant crypto stash of his own, but the evidence otherwise is all circumstantial. The more compelling evidence is how much effort seems to go in to discrediting Dorian

62091923
>>62091693
It's always funny to watch 4cuck brownoids and hapa mongs seethe at the fact that whites made yet another world-changing invention while their own score, as a race, continues to sit at 1 kek

62091931
He already denied it

62091938
It's so obviously Hal Finney

item.title: WAR IS OVER — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WAR IS OVER

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-04-08 12:23 UTC

OP

god bless the great people of Iran may they please accept America's unconditional surrender.

sorry to any liquid niggers that got liquidated (heh), but it's officially over.

Early replies (first 3)

62089549
meanwhile, niggas in tel aviv are still getting text alerts to shelter at 3am

62089565
>>62089549
good thing no human beings live there

62089657
>>62089532
how long until someone find out those odd 5-minute early shorts on oil and bets on Trump TACOing?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62089687
You got two extreme easy short entries, as I said, now just keep rolling over shorts
The trajectory for energy hasnt changed
10-30 dollar barrel in the next 10 years. likely lower as the market is a bunch of front running retards

62089731
>>62089687
>The trajectory for energy hasnt changed
>10-30 dollar barrel in the next 10 years.
why

62089815
>>62089731
In one word, socialists.
the dead of the human species. The idiots have managed what 200,000 years of humanity hasn't managed, a natural deflation that is going to last between 80 - 500 years.
Less people, less demand for everything, less profits. Energy, food, finished goods, services, credit.... Short everything

62089911
>>62089532
play shitty games win shitty prizes

62090236
>>62089815
wtf based socialists saving humanity

62090248
>>62090236
>saved
I strongly suggest you go ahead and kill yourself now. Put your spine where your words are

62090255
>>62089532
>I bought XOM calls the day before the largest one day drop since 2008 and it’s down the same amount now just in the after hours a week later
>I sold USO because it wasn’t going up and it shot up the same day XOM tanked
This market is impossible.

62090259
>>62090248
Everything you mentioned was good for the soul of mankind, freeing us from the grasp of Satan’s fiery grip and rising back to the realm of God

62090273
>>62090259
And how is it good?
Ah you think "the others" will disappear and YOU can profit. And how are you going to not disappear you little shitter? Have you ever klled a man? Ever robbed somebody, ever beaten the shit out of somebody? No? Thought so. You die first

62090278
The cave is opened. The false world crumbles and humanity is set free from the shadows. Praise Him.

62090517
>>62090259
TRVKE

62090831
>>62090273
le seethe is strong in this one

anyway, socialists based as usual

62090893
>>62081135

62090917
the futures was fake and gay
when war end we will print new-ones, with IraNIAN TRANSFER TAX INCLUDED

62091887
>>62090831
And that's how I know that the party is over. When socialist religious scum starts to party about deflation, they went went full retard and true believer. Socialist rome is burning down, Moises Hess and Marx rome is dead

item.title: I have thousands of dollars invested in OIL and have made really good money...But with Trump now threatening to kill all… — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I have thousands of dollars invested in OIL and have made really good money...But with Trump now threatening to kill all…

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-04-08 12:28 UTC

OP

I have thousands of dollars invested in OIL and have made really good money...But with Trump now threatening to kill all of Iran, a part of me lowkey hope it happens so that I make a fuckload of money and don't lose it all, but at the same time its like nah man NAH this is weeeird bruh this is weird bruh like this feels evil man..Am I going to get bad karma from this? Would this be considered blood money?
Part of me hopes something big happens because I want to make more money, but another part of me is thinking that I am a terrible person profiting off of human suffering...Should I just stop giving a fuck bros, like am I overthinking this?

Early replies (first 3)

62088410
>>62088395
Sell it for bitcoin. Trumps are retard working for Israel

62089934
>>62088395
How ya feelin champ?

62089959
>>62088395
Too late retard. You got rugged, thanks for playing. See ya next cycle bud no refunds btw

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62090086
>>62088395
Similar situation here. I am actually indifferent to the ceasefire, not mad or anything. I guess my cope is that I don't want these things to happen, but I have no influence so why don't I ride the wave.

62090186
>>62088395
>>62090086
You're supposed to be invested in oil as a hedge against other markets. You're supposed to make money regardless of whether high oil prices kill equities or not. You'd be losing a lot of money in oil right now, but making enough money in other markets such that it doesn't matter

62090217
>>62090186
>You're supposed to make money regardless of whether high oil prices kill equities or not
I did/will, but I saw oil as making me liquid money to be rotated into the my other positions.

62091876
>>62088395
I voted for this

62091896
Hope you got out after you made this thread LOL

62091902
>>62088395
Your making money will nearly always lead to someone else's misery. Even if they didnt throw in and lose they will be pissed that you have it so it doesn't really matter. Just do what you have to do OP.

item.title: Aside from taking the entire country then owning all the oil this is the best ending we could have gotten. — 28 replies — 34.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Aside from taking the entire country then owning all the oil this is the best ending we could have gotten.

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-04-08 11:48 UTC

OP

Aside from taking the entire country then owning all the oil this is the best ending we could have gotten.
>israel left to fight alone with 0 chance of them screwing up the ceasefire because they are not involved
>china lost cheapies on oil as it will now be available on the open market
>iran raped to hell and back plus they are going to lose uranium enrichment or get killed again
>europe likely cucked with a 2 million fee while the US doesn't use that as we get our oil locally and now Venezuela
>europe exposed as cucks who won't help us so we will now leave the bases and take our weapons which they will have to buy from us later as they more than double their military budget in order to just be the same
>US finished off the war with a legendary story about saving a pilot and also showing how the world values old tech rather than their old soldiers
>several generations of iranian leadership lost, their entire navy and airforce destroyed, once they leave the tunnels we will know where they were and can attack them if needed in the future.
>bobos liquidated
>israel devastated by strikes and is hated by the world

Early replies (first 3)

62090777
THAT'S MY PRESIDENT

62090866
>actually here's why losing India is actually a good thing for the British empire

62090876
>>62090752
We will attack iran later. This was all a carefully choreographed move by AIPAC/Mossad or as I've recently taking to calling them, GNU plus AIPAC/Mossad....

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62090883
>actually here’s why the barbarians invading Rome is good for the romans

62090886
>>62090752
You realise the 2m fee will raise oil prices for Americans too since the oil on those tankers feeds into the price paid by the buyer and oil is a global commodity... Right?

62090895
>>62090886
Isn't that less than 1% tax on profit, considering tankers hold 2-3 million oil barrels

62090916
>>62090886
you realise that iran being allowed to sell oil will drop the price more than the 2 million fee will raise right
You do realize that increased investment in venezuela will increasingly lower oil prices right?

62090923
>>62090752
>actually losing control of the suez canal is a good thing for europe

62090936
>>62090752
Wut?

62090937
>>62090936
this and im all outta cum

62090976
>>62090923
not a yuropoor anon those fuckers deserve this for not helping
>>62090936
The US won again and everyone who didn't help is getting punished also israel is still getting fucked

62090988
>>62090976
>reference the exact point at which europe stopped being superpower several decades ago
>american thinks im talking about something that just happened
Holy shit why is american education so fucking bad?

62090991
>>62090976
If it makes u sleep.

62090995
>>62090988
Europe isn't America doe

62091002
>>62090752
NUCLEAR TREAD WAS USED
PUTIN SAVE US AGAIN

62091003
>>62090988
Why the fuck would I bother to learn about european history?

62091007
>>62091003
America is the one that gave the suez canal back to egypt. It was the moment that established america as the new superpower over europe. How the fuck do you not know this? If anything it's more relevant to america than europe.

62091018
>>62091007
Everyone knows WW2 was the true cementing of the US as a superpower not the mention of the canal that likely was a single paragraph in the history books
You only learned it in depth because it was your humiliation
>>62091002
Putin doesn't benefit from peace with iran, oil prices will drop now

62091433
>>62090866
The gulf arabs was the only countries that would realistically be removed from the map if Trump went ahead with targeting power plants

62091555
>>62090916
Both statements cannot be true, first you said europe will lose out and then the next you say the oil will be cheaper. So what is it?

62091576
>>62090916
(You)r futures will expire earlier

62091590
>>62090752
That's why I voted for him
>t. didn't vote

62091735
>isr*el left to do what they always do, instead of getting volley-nuked into glass like they should be; somehow this is a victory
>china hoarding metals and doesn't give a shit, actively setting up mass solar farms
>it won't matter if iran opens the strait he caused the closure of, because he won't be able to get his oil anyway if all the processing infrastructure is fucked, even funnier because he didn't refill the reserve before randomly declaring war in the first place and started funding the regime he's bombing to get fuel for it
>the bulk fee will probably only be for burgerland and allies because iran outright said they just want to give america a spanking
>no one helped him because none of this made sense and was the stupidest shit ever, he also started threatening them and starting a trade war with everyone for no good reason right before begging for help like a cuck
>the war is still going, he declared it won a month ago then threw a tantrum over the weekend and is now realizing he has nothing, also the administration got fooled by an AI slop pic of a pilot being rescued lmao
>successfully killed an ailing old man that had a few months left due to cancer, but also a bunch of schoolgirls and a music academy because they decided to use Claude and didn't bother to check if the targeting data was correct and up to date
>>related by the way https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/01/claude_code_source_leak_privacy_nightmare/
>resident richfags and their friends encouraging everyone to buy more Bitcoin to support their libertarian fantasy larp to pump the numbers so they can cash out
>isr*el is indeed hated by the world
Miss anything?

62091736
>>62090752
Retarded spin doctoring won't change this being shartmerica's Suez moment.

62091739
>>62091735
So this is what TDS looks like

62091741
>>62090752
What a retard you are.
I can't even believe it.
U.S. army troops are in Southern Israel manning radars and Patriot missile batteries.

62091745
>>62090876
>or as I've recently taking to calling them, GNU plus AIPAC/Mossad....
Kek. Okay (((Richard Stallman)))

62091756
>>62091739
Holy cope. Here, I'll make one for you since you're sensitive and/or a bot:
>everything is perfect and fine and Trump deserves all the medals and is definitely god emperor like preddit says
>the US is winning in every way, the price of everything is the lowest it's ever been and there's 500 million more jobs, heil hortler
>you are the bestest boy too and will definitely get a demure virgin white tradwife and a billion dollars from the government and live on a mansion homestead with 300 kids and a bunch of plantation slaves or something

just put the fries in the bag

item.title: Why aren’t you holding BTC? — 13 replies — 13.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren’t you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-04-08 11:38 UTC

OP

If you’re still watching or waiting to buy BTC, why not do it now?
>I guess you’re wasting time, or maybe you just prefer altcoins. What’s your reason?

Early replies (first 3)

62087977
>>62087937
I only watch it to laugh at Saylor and you bitsteinbaggies. Not buying digital nothing.

62087993
>>62087937
Waiting for sub60K

62087997
>>62087937
I don't have enough money for it to make me rich. Alts have a good chance of vastly outperforming BTC during the next big pump. I've decided to take the potential for a 10x over the guarantee of a 2x.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62088037
>>62087937

I am, though.

>>62087977
>>62087993
>>62087997

These anons have learning disabilities.

62088069
>>62087937
because btc has no worth and is purely for retards

62088107
>>62087937
Buy orders set at $40K. See you in October.

62088185
>>62088069

Why are gold fags always in Bitcoin threads showing off their low IQs?

62090867
>>62087977
You just don't have money haaaaa

62091414
>>62087993
Not gonna happen

62091478
gimme money

62091551
>>62088185
because they're poor forever

62091723
>>62088069
Consider this: the financial system's shenanigans are now big enough for speculation to make inherent worth. Its value, like all other currency, is that people see value in it. Except instead of the value being "oooh shiny" (silver, gold) or "hmmm tasty" (rice, cows, corn taxes, etc) it's now "hmmmm this might go up in the future if speculation drives it higher".

Tldr: it's arbitrary like everything else and does have value

62091734
I like that crypto trades 24/7 so if I need to sell some to buy a mcburger at 1 am I don’t need to wait for gay wallstreet to ring a bell

item.title: >He STILL hasn't bought gold At this point you just don't want money lol — 2 replies — 8.0/hr

OP

62091903

>biz spammed with buying propaganda
You know what to do.

62091904
Gold is for jewellery, I want to buy my wife a gold necklace but you fags are bidding it up too much its getting out of hand, hope you fags lose all your money so I can buy gold jewellery at a decent price again

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: There are NO jobs left — 76 replies — 59.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
There are NO jobs left

Open thread • Replies: 76 • Updated: 2026-04-08 11:20 UTC

OP

> 8 in 10 jobs posted online are FAKE
> those 2 in 10 jobs that aren't fake are all looking for either a buddy, family member, internal, senior level, or a literal unicorn for their positions

It's truly the end times now, isn't it?

Early replies (first 3)

62084288
Now you see why war is necessary. That will create a ton of jobs that you can do.

62084296
>>62084281
There's plenty of jobs it just the wages won't go up. I quit my shitty QC job and got another one a week later.

62084378
>>62084281
yeah there literally arent.. all these companies keep firing tens of thousands of people and we barely create any jobs

even dog shit mcwage jobs are impossible to get. Before covid you put in 10 applications and got a 3 stage interview into offer. Now its all fucked up

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62087207
>>62087172
>just let the government literally probe your asshole and be subject to random drug screenings, continuous surveillence and FBI(r) Background Checks(tm) continuously for a job worse than anything commercial.
if you're such a fucking loser, than ya, go for it. but having to report to the government just because you go to shitaly or some shit is fucking dumb, and again, they are watching you continuously with their Palantir bullshit spyware.

62087227
>>62087172
Bro I have secret clearance and it doesn’t do shit desu

62087240
>>62087227
grim.

62087281
>>62084281
Steps:
1) Get a job
2) Sell all your crypto and get a money market fund at 4% per year.
Poor people shouldn't buy crypto!!!!

62087291
>>62084281
>It's truly the end times now, isn't it?
So when people declared "the end times" for the past 10+ years, what were you doing?
There's no jobs, no houses, no white people, no fucking anything.

62087377
>>62084281
White harder.

62087528
>>62084281
Stop using sites like Indeed, they keep up closed positions to pump their application numbers. There will be less fake postings on the companies' websites.

62087560
>>62087227
is there any way to get one that doesn't involve getting it sponsored by a company? i want one and meet the baseline requirements but haven't gotten any job that would sponsor it yet

62087590
>>62084394
fucking lady won't answer the phone at my doctor's office I don't know if she's Asian or Latina I think she's a hybrid
Would/10 tho if I ever get in there and get her alone and don't hnnnnnggh

62087707
>>62087172
>get a TS/SCI security clearance
Well obviously do this if you can but if it was easy everyone would and the jobs wouldn't pay so much.

62087766
>>62087227
Going from secret to TS/SCI instantly moved me from $85k a year to $140k a year and that was literally 16 years ago. If I still had a TS/SCI I'd make $225k a year easily at an easy job where I'd take 2 hour gym lunch breaks every day and work out with literal Navy Seals like I used to.

62087805
cleared world was funny, so many people did literally nothing all day and got paid six figure salaries with top tier benefits. ur in a windowless office day with no phone so it kinda blows though

62087955
>>62087766
Yea but to get TS u need a justification and that means generally a managerial role or principal role

62088163
>>62087805
I don't know how these people mentally cope with their own existence. The idea of willingly going to work every day for years and contributing absolutely nothing is just insane to me. I used to be in that world too, even as an "executive level" (junior manager), and I couldn't bear it. So I quit and now I'm happily doing the job of 5 people.

I often dream of working on one of those big projects full of competent people like the Manhattan project, cracking Enigma, or anything in early NASA. I know I have the skills for it, but I don't think those projects exist any more. The world has become so enshittified that everyone's just trying to keep their head down and avoid attention.

62088237
>>62088163
I could never figure it out myself, there were sometimes weeks were there was nothing to do but everyone had to show up for 8 hours and pretend to be busy with stuff. Soul sucking... Only lasted a few years, tried both civ/con roles. Skills regressed heavily. Glad you found something meaningful. It would be amazing to work on a project like that, but yeah most people just want the paycheck and to keep their head down.

62088559
>>62087207

>Get spied on by Palantir for free

>Get spied on by Palantir and by Caren in HR while you work at Nabisco company for $45,000 and get fired

>Get spied on by Palantir and your federal agency in a do-nothing comfy ClearanceJob for $200,000

Hmmmmmmmm

>inb4 "But weed, dude!"

62088598
>>62087955
>Yea but to get TS u need a justification and that means generally a managerial role or principal role
Nah just go for places like DIA, NRO, NGA where all employees get TS/SCI, even the janitors.

62088633
>>62084387
Gold

62088645
>>62085265
They're not slaves, they're conquerors.

62089048
>>62087560
>is there any way to get one that doesn't involve getting it sponsored by a company?
things like PALACE ACQUIRE and intelligence agency summer internships
these get neverserved college students a TS/SCI from scratch
>>62087707
>get a TS/SCI security clearance
>Well obviously do this if you can but if it was easy everyone would and the jobs wouldn't pay so much.
it's generally not easy to be sponsored by a company/hired directly by the feds, but once you are, the security clearance investigation itself is easy to pass. there are hundreds (or possibly thousands) of convicted felons who then went on to get TS/SCI.

62089220
>>62084281
>Get an education.
>Still no jobs.
>Covid ends.
>Still no jobs.
>Get rid of the immigrants.
>Still no jobs.
>Jobs reports announce hundreds of thousands of new jobs.
>Still no jobs.
When I say jobs, I mean real jobs with real pay and benefits.

>>62084288
>Xir thinks war is still done like it was in Vietnam.
lol
No, war is nothing but expensive if you're not a country that stuck 30-40 years in the past. America hinted at it in Afghanistan and Ukraine set it in stone against Russia. It's taught the entire world how future wars are going to be fought.
Drones, drones, drones.

62090040
>>62087766
tfw diagnosed schizo so can't get TS/SCI
yes my main board is /pol/

62090429
>>62084281
I have 3 WFH jobs and I am trying to get a third one. If I manage to get one, then there's something else going on, you gotta be doing something wrong.

62091311
>>62084281
I can't find a fucking job.

62091326
>>62087805
Looking back, the niggers with 2 full time jobs were the smart ones. One during the day and another at night.
Get while the getting is good.
Niggers understand.
Always be nigging.
I watched a nigger come into work, eat dinner, then sleep all night for years.

62091333
>>62088163
>>62088237
Because some people don't come from privilege so they know what it's like to be poor and being poor is WAY FUCKING WORSE than getting a 6 figure salary to do a fake job.

62091402
>>62085296
>Eastern Europe
>White
My old dog was Whiter.

62091633
>>62090429
having 1 job is embarrassing enough -- and you're bragging about having 2 or 3?

62091635
>>62084281
I'm not even looking for one.

62091695
>>62091402
Weird cope Juan but okay. Only 8% of Eastern Europeans have brown eyes.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 271 replies — 52.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 271 • Updated: 2026-04-08 07:29 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62078039
>>62077075
You are not in India

62078216
>>62078039
This is my hand >>62008295 young Vishnu. This was taken directly from Mumbai, the city of DOT.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62078593
Dont selling my dotty anymore

62078595
75

62078631
Depressing thread

62078789
>>62078216
Fake

62079572
>>62078631
Well, I've already got my 11.5k DOT that Gaving promised me and every day this thread stay alive I get an additional 150DOT. Doesn't really seem depressing to me

>>62078593
>>62078595
100$ END OF BULL RUN

>>62078789
I am brown as chocolate itself. I am a true Mumbai warrior

Thank you for keeping the thread alive my Mumbai warriors. You are feeding your village. This is a cooperative effort. The mumbai boys strike AGAIN


Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62079598
This is the most damaging thread for dot in history

62079614
>>62079598
Read this >>62069282. We're playing this whole board. They're eating right out of our hands. First we make them believe that DOT is held by the GLAMOROUS citizens of Mumbai. Then at the same time we pocket the money from Gavin Wood for keeping the thread alive. AND at the same time we SHORT IT TO 0$. The whole city of Mumbai is in on it. INDIA SUPER POWER BY 2020 wasn't a lie. It's a FACT for 6 years already.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62079649
>>62079614
But is the tech good?

62079750
>>62079649
Literally THE BEST chain currently. And no, I'm not just making shit up. It literally does what everyone else is trying to do. Once JAM + NOMT hit it'll be able to do MILLIONS of TPS (not just value tx) while doing continous compute, while being 100% decentalized, while the tx will be CHEAP, while having NATIVE interoperability, while being able to control other chains and never leave DOT, ... This projects is THE project and people are still buying meme coins. It's UNFATHOMABLE.

62080914
I hope you guys smell the bottom. I can smell the bottom of the Vishnu mindchain. I hope you're going in HARD and DEEP. I hope you guys are PENETRATING THE BOTTOM of the market. I hope you're bouncing up and down on that bottom. I hope you're eating that bottom out and scooping it up. Mmmm, that delicious Vishnu bottom. It has a great taste inside my mouth. I hope you guys are enjoying it as much as I am. I'll leave some cream for you so it tastes even better.

62080937
>>62079649
DOT is literally the most superior blockchain out there. Everything that other people promise and don't deliver DOT already has. Everything that other people lie about and can't do DOT can do. The really BIG upgrade we're waiting on is JAM and NOMT this year
Read more here >>62026407 and >>62056612 and >>62056570. NOMT + JAM scale the network to more a few million or maybe 10-20m TPS. We currently have the highest recorded TPS on a live network (https://data.parity.io/spammening). 143.3k TPS on a live network (NOT TEST NET BULLSHITERY like the other chains are showcasing). This was done one 23 of the 100 cores that are available. So this was only 23% of the full capacity. Some cores are already sold to other chains therefore we can't do a full test. But you can guesstimate the numbers, right? And since the last spammening the throughput has gone up. We could now probably do like 250k TPS from 23 cores or something like that. Just look at the "Performance Charts" on the page I linked. The fees were literally almost always below 0.05$. The fees were around 0.03 to 0.015$ even when the network handled 140k TPS. Do you remember how Solana halted the network literally 8x due to it being congested and shit like that? That can't happen on DOT. The average finality was 16.58s. The thing is though, we'll soon be getting 500ms blocks. When the tests were conducted the block times were still 12s? Now they're 6s and soon they'll be 500ms (Basti blocks). A lot is happening on DOT this year!
Also if you look at the stats the network has been growing over time https://data.parity.io/parachain-stats?selection-mode=include&frequency=daily

62080949
>>62079649
Also combine that with the new tokenomics and the long term vision for the project... I really don't see this project becoming irrelevant to be honest. It simply JUST FUCKING DOES WHAT IT'S SUPPOSED TO DO. The other L2s are behind and they won't catch up ever in my opinion. Once JAM hits DOT will be AT LEAST 5 to 10 years ahead of all the major L2s and L1s. It's not even comparable to be honest. People are blind to not see it. Sooner or later people will wake up and realize "OMG, I've been a degenerate memecoin gambler while I could buy the Vishnu of the blockchain".
Simply put, this project does what others promise and don't deliver. DOT has already DELIVERED and is DELIVERING even more amazing things that no other chain has and if they do they don't package it like DOT does. Sure ICP has continous compute, but does it have native interoperability and can host L2s and smart contracts and has 500ms block times and is decentralized and has shared security to his L2s and has the best governance system in crypto and has over a few million TPS and has ... and has ... Bro, it's fucking impossible to beat DOT on technical superiority. Some day people will realize that they can't build a new use case on the other chains. That they're LIMITING THEMSELVES by staying on shit like ARB or POL or some other fucking shit like that. A few months back Polkadot Asset Hub launched and people can simply copy paste EVM code to DOT and it'll work. We're currently waiting for this referendum (https://polkadot.polkassembly.io/referenda/1864) to pass in order for Uniswap and Safe to launch on Asset Hub. Other major projects will probably follow. Should pass any day now. Slow and steady wins the race

62081930
>>62079750
>>62080914
>>62080937
>>62080949

Why should we believe some one from India

62082053
>>62081930
Truth is not to be believed. Truth is to be seen. Just try the chain out. And look at the data. I've provided you with plenty of evidence. You SHOULD NOT believe me. The essence of crypto is what? DYOR. Do Your Own Research. We really did forget our roots, didn't we?

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62082374
>>62081930
I actually applaud you to be honest. Anon, I'm proud of you. Thank you for having CRITICAL thinking. We need more people like you. And again DYOR and you WILL come to similar if not EXACTLY THE SAME conclusions. I dare you, I double dare you, I triple dare you.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62084668
100$ end of bull run

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62085029
75.
y2029

62085250
>>62026429
imagine being really hard to develop on, because your system uses a particularly confusing superset of rust, and then scrambling to make an EVM to attract more developers. no sympathy for rust faggots.

62086293
>>62085250
>because your system uses a particularly confusing superset of rust
Help me understand what you mean by
>a particularly confusing superset of rust
Rust is a programming language, sure. And how would one use a particular subset of it? Like what exactly do you mean by that?

62086622
>>62085250
Even if you just google "Does polkadot use a subset of rust or rust?"
It'll say "
Polkadot uses Rust—specifically, modern, full-featured Rust—rather than a restricted subset of the language.
Polkadot is built on the Substrate framework, which is designed from the ground up in Rust to ensure memory safety and high performance, critical for blockchain infrastructure.
"
So, I have no clue where you're getting your information from. The thing is I'm a programmer and what you said seems so akward. You're basically trying to imply that you can take just a subset of the functionality of Rust. A "confusing" subset of a programming language. Like what? You only take the else statement without the if? Can you elaborate? I guess you probably can't because either you're spreading FUD or you've been manipulated into something that isn't real.

62087253
>>61932834
Not even Larp Davis shills this gay little coin anymore.

62087258
>>61948998
>We've got a true nazi over here.
See? Nobody cares.

62087587
>>62087253
>>62087258
So, what's your point exactly? Thank you for the bump

62087811
>>62087587
My point is, Lark Davis has forsaken you. It's ogre.

62088494
>>62087811
Ok, that's great you informed us. And so what's your point again? I still don't see it.

62088504
>>62087811
Like, how does that do anything for me or anyone? Like what's your point in regard to how that affects DOT? Or anything? I really don't see a connection

62089608
I hope you guys slurped the bottom. We're cooking now, boys.


Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62091358
1.33>13 DECEMBER

item.title: So, now that assblaster was confirmed controlled opposition, and LINK holders have become "the Cuckolds of Crypto"... — 53 replies — 12.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
So, now that assblaster was confirmed controlled opposition, and LINK holders have become "the Cuckolds of Crypto"...

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-04-08 08:24 UTC

OP

So, now that assblaster was confirmed controlled opposition, and LINK holders have become "the Cuckolds of Crypto"... shouldn't we have a look at what he told us *not* to buy?

He tried very hard to steer us away from one coin in particular back in 2018.

They wanted you out.

Early replies (first 3)

62077049
well he was right about raiblocks

62077089
>>62077049
Only in the sense that he was "right" about LINK. It's price did go up, yet it achieved nothing. Sergey met Trump and still nobody cares about smartcontracts. LINK had every chance to succeed but failed.

>Controlled opposition is a political or strategic tactic where a governing body, organization, or elite group secretly supports, infiltrates, or manages its own opposition to manipulate dissent, control narratives, and prevent real challenges to the status quo

A functional stateless currency would challenge the status quo, and now it's been "over 5 years under the radar".

Watch how long this thread stays up.

62077098
>>62077000
>>62077049
>>62077089
Bullish for XRP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62078711
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62078735
He even admitted his intentions in one of his final posts.

This ARG goes DEEP.

62078857
>>62078703
everyone needs to see this

62078921
>>62078618
But didn't you originally intend to make money?
Or are you like a sportsball fan that pays the team regardless of outcome and don't share in the winnings?

62079599
>>62078703
cuckolds of crypto.

It was there the whole time.

62079836
>>62078703
There it is folks.

"Cuckolds of crypto" was planned all along.

If you're still holding LINK after seeing this... well, you deserve what's next.

62079965
>>62078735
Link was .30-.40 when he posted this.

62079978
>>62077648
You mean the schizo posts about the referenced world vs the whatever the fuck he called the “new world”

62080163
>>62077915
that's a lot of manufactured horseshit for
>be at the right place at the right time

62080436
>>62079836
pool's closed / thanks for keeping this a link board.

62080485
>>62077089
Thread's still up and likely will be for days because this is a dead board, the irony of you making fun of one group of shitcoin holders for invoking hidden lore/"its a sekret" terminology and doing the same thing in your thread, nobody fucking cares about xrp you boomer faggot.
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62080493
>12 pbtid
>about raiblocks
Hmmm

62080684
>>62080485
>this is a dead board
It's a dead site, dude. It was fucking swamped with non-whites years ago and they all just go to /b/, /v/ and /pol/ for their le epic psyops and bait threads. All the average intelligence white people that used to be on this website are gone. I dunno where they went. A lot to Twitter, I guess.

62080692
>>62080684
>twitter
Yeah I think so. I’m not smart but I am white and I can’t believe it but I spend a far more amount of time on x now than 4chins. I’ve been here every waking minute since 2011 and never thought this place could be so fucking boring and gay.

62080695
>>62080684
That's true, probably why adem feels so at home here.

62081569
>>62078703
Assblaster convinced /biz/ to CHAINLINK themselves to a SINGULAR TREE and then cuckolded them!

The cuckolds of crypto was planned out, years in advance.

62081749
>>62078703
>>62081569
This is educational

/biz/ got psyopped, no doubt about it

>A SINGULAR TREE
>TONIGHT, STRAP IN

Inverse Assblaster

62081755
>>62080485
thread's still up because I didn't use "the N word"

Any threads that explicitly state Raiblock's new name or ticker quickly get taken out.

Don't believe me?

It's all in the archives

https://warosu.org/biz/thread/59678970
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/59678347
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/59671138
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/59600393
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/59538201

62082372
>>62078703
It's game over for stinkies

62082722
>>62078703
BOOOOOOOOOOOM

cuckolds of crypto!

62082884
>>62078703
remember when hillary talking about pepe was a big deal

62083016
>>62080684
why exactly did this site die off so quickly

x is awful and they're always changing the algorithms
plebbit hasn't been useable for at least 6 years due to insane moderation
every traditional forum is dead

where the hell did everyone go

62083040
>>62083016
Crypto isn't exciting since 2017 ended

Why?

Because people forgot what we were doing here to begin with

People forgot about Raiblocks

Tonight, they will be reminded

62084453
>>62083016
Moot was a spineless coward and Hiroshimoot doesn't give two shits about anything so long as the ad money stays flowing, so White men left for Twitter, a website that actually has influence on things.

62084638
>>62084453
>muh tweets are makin' a difference
they're just as useless as 4chan posts

62085762
>>62084453
yeah that makes sense
just sucks because the format here is so much easier to read

62085897
fuck all stinkies hate em all think they suck\fuck all stinkies hate em all think they suck\fuck all stinkies hate em all think they suck

62087388
>>62078703
Cuckolds of crypto

62087403
hug all stinkies love em all think theyre great\hug all stinkies love em all think theyre great\hug all stinkies love em all think theyre great

62091446
>>62078703

u
c
k
o
l
d

o

item.title: The US dollars was 70% of the world's reserve currency in 2016. — 106 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US dollars was 70% of the world's reserve currency in 2016.

Open thread • Replies: 106 • Updated: 2026-04-08 03:49 UTC

OP

The US dollars was 70% of the world's reserve currency in 2016. It's 39% now.

Early replies (first 3)

62074981
>>62074965
lol no, the financial bust part is the great depression, the revolution and wars is the period between the end of ww2 and 2008, debt and restructuring was 2008 onwards

62074987
Seems like a silly unfounded chart given that gold were the main reserves before the dollar.

62075007
>>62074981
>the revolution and wars is the period between the end of ww2 and 1971, debt and restructuring was 1971 to 2008(2020 maybe?), new world order from then on

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62084400
>>62083989
No. Control over 85% of global oil reserves does that. Stable coins are just a failed attempt at forcing private actors by legislative action to buy sovereign bond ponzis and by that making their own business model even more questionable of a ponzi as it already is. Crapto has no yield, its a pure greater fool ponzi scheme

62084571
>>62074965
so glad the lowest iq mutts got together to torch their fourth world shithole just as the rest of the world leaves them behind for good
starting new wars won't save you, in fact it only accelerates the collapse

62084585
>>62084571
We don't intend to rock the boat, we intend to sink it.

62084617
>>62075553
What the other guys said but we got a good exchange rate for the most part until recently

62084640
>>62075131
If China wanted to rule the world, they would ally with their neighbors & win easily.
Instead they war their neighbors every chance they get.
Which is why theyll never be more than 2nd rate.

62084641
>>62075904
he wants russian resources.
Its smart but not realistic. Maybe after Putin dies things can change.

62084650
>>62075553
It creats demand for dollars around the world. That keeps the price of dollars higher than it otherwise would be since new supply of dollars can be sent to other countries in exchange for manufacturered goods. This means the US doesn't need to have a massive manufacturing base at home which means less pollution and in general high stress and health risk jobs. Instead the US economy can function by basically finding various mechanisms to just hand the US population money which they can spend and eventually the money finds its way abroad. This creates a large service economy where most people don't actually do any "real work" like making shit. Now their job is just a way to inject money into the domestic economy so that it can then be sent over seas and then those people can use that money to buy things like US bonds(both corporate and government), oil, US stocks and other things that are traded in dollars. Jobs end up being less stressful, you work fewer hours, and there's lots of room to climb up since career ladders are just social structures to hand out money rather than actually give you real responsibility. There's still plenty of real jobs of course, but an insane amount of US jobs are the kind where you just sit at a computer and read emails and then send another email to someone else and that person tells the floormanager of a factory to tell their immigrant labor that there's overtime this weekend.

So Americans enjoy a high standard of living even compared to other developed countries. People own cars, get cheap fuel, imported goods are cheap, there's options to save with high returns and so on. If the dollar stopped being the reserve currency all the dollars that's currently floating around in the world economy would have little use outside the US and they'd flow back into the domestic economy. The dollar loses most of its value and the standard of living of Americans drops since they're losing access to foreign goods.

62084652
>>62082104
What future?

62084837
>>62075652
This and also the US can use it's global financial control as a weapon, which is what's pissing other countries off right now and causing de-dollarisation. But having the world reserve currency can also cause the whole world economy to dip like with 2008;

62087251
>>62074965
The big question is what basket of currency should I hodl when I sell muh Bittycorns. Swiss Franc? UAE Dirham? NOT gbp or eur those commie basterds are so cooked lmao

62087273
>>62087251
If you are in the US, only dollar
if you are outside the US, 90% dollar 10% whatever hyperinflated peripheral paper the treasury of the central planning committee that declared it legal tender shits out on your territory with likely 0 energy resources, 0 relevant manufacturing
>b-but I am in Chinka
>the sweatshop of the world
you definitely don't want to keep any yuan
>w-what about shiny rocks
lmfao

62087292
>>62075699
lol think you mean the cause of my phat stack

62087386
>>62075026
He's still a midwit whose firm has been sheltering closeted faggots for decades.

62087393
>>62087386
>ad blog drivel
not better than the 20th century mongoloid that tried to play epistemic authority. In fact exactly the same
same noose for this kind of scammers
ignoring is not enough, death is too good

62087426
>>62074965
This graph is not applicable to the US since it found itself in a position of power almost by accident after WW2 and managed to scam the whole world by soft-defaulting on its USD debt and "temporarily" ending Bretton Woods. This has nothing to do with actual empires. What you are witnessing is the return to the mean of the longest-running Ponzi in history.

62087571
>>62087426
Entire point of Dallios argument is that all leading economies eventually borrow more than they can afford to pay and it plays out mostly the same every time. They start with sound money. Then they switch to fractional reseves and then absolute fiat as a temporary measures which ends up being permanent due to how effective they are in the short term. Paper wealth appears to grow, debts pile up, then they reach some new emergency and have no option left and then the system implodes. Lather, rinse, repeat.

62087661
>>62087571
No that is not his entire point. Dalio is trying to derive a historical pattern out of a sample of one that cannot be applied retrospectively. The USD fiat Ponzi is unprecedented and financial "geniuses" like him directly benefitted from it but will frame its implosion as some kind of cyclical inevitability to deflect from the inherent and talmudic depravity of the whole system.

62087679
>>62087661
True.
yet here you are posting JYT retardation.
Its time to kill your entire lot. No exceptions.

62087791
>>62087679
>homosexual ESL spergs out when his fellow queers are exposed

62087814
>>62074965
The people that make these charts are legitimately retarded. I could debunk all of this.

62087946
>>62082104
>Where is the future then?
Whoever has the most brainwashed and loyal army willing to commit unimaginable crimes against the human race once all the chips are down.

62087958
>>62074965
white people were 95% of the world's population 100 years ago and now theyre only 5% and its not because we started actually counting how many jeets sand niggers and niggers and spics there actually are?

62087968
>>62074965
the world printed a lot more money than we did

62089626
>>62087791
are you projecting you geezer? You are the one bringing an ad blog screencap as an authoritative source that has a reach under 5%. This isnt the 1980s. Nobody gives two shits what any jewrnalists shits out. There are fucking only fans models with higher reach than the NYT, WSJ and other adblogs from 50 years ago

62090246
>>62075109
lol get ready to learn Chinese or your social credit score will fall

62090256
>>62090246
>chinese
chang, you have neither energy, now after the Iran shit, not even by proxy, you have no arid land, you have no fresh water and your export economy is dead, choking on over production with a shadow unemployment that goes toward 30%. The chinks are the last that pose any competition to the dollar. You are in a worse situation than Japan in 1998

62090275
>>62075109
BWAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHhahahahahahahahahahahahahaha ahahahahahah
Haaa
Ha ha
good one ano

62090282
>>62075897
What soft power?
Germany's vision of buying Russian oil in exchange for peace turned out to be a shit show

62090374
>>62077415
This fucking retard looked at a single chart on a single asset and extrapolated kek. Meanwhile in reality the marketcap for USD assets is like 20x as large as gold lol

62090837
>>62084640
>china
>war
pick one

item.title: >HE BOUGHT FUCKING OIL — 23 replies — 9.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
>HE BOUGHT FUCKING OIL

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-04-08 10:10 UTC

OP

>HE BOUGHT FUCKING OIL

Early replies (first 3)

62087549
>>62087540
LUBRICANT?

62087618
Seems to be the most jewish asset on teh planet desu senpai. Physical barrels are going for $150 a barrel now, but the niggers in charge of the prices are only pricing it at $70 a barrel for the next few months??

So if you question it, people say the strait will be opened and everything will be fine, but at this point tons of petrochemical plants are getting blown up left and right. Its not even about the strait anymore....but for some reason the price doesn't really take off like it should.

62087841
>>62087540
BABY OIL DIDDY BLUD

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62087921
>>62087618
It could be the market pricing in covid style lockdowns to reduce consumption.

62087922
>>62087618
The trick is that there is no such thing as a free market and there never will be.

62087932
>>62087540
Better than dog coins

62087936
>>62087922
Sure, but if you're underpricing a real physical thing, you're going to have to start rationing it. Also you're probably going to see a black market form if the right measures aren't taken.

62087973
>>62087540
if you got in on it early in the fiasco then good for you. buying now? you must be nuts when the hormuz is barely a fraction of a percent of commerce. they'll have to make paper oil cheap again eventually.

62088130
>>62087540
I've owned oil for 6 years. Get on my fucking level.

62088140
>>62087540
Shut the fuck up you moron. It's going to $200 whether you like it or not.

62088605
>>62087540
>bought USOY at the literal pico-top
>signs of taco tonight with rumours of ceasefire
I guess I will be averaging down. There's no way oil doesn't reach $200 this year
>>62087973
It's not barely a fraction. Jet fuel price skyrocketed already and 7% of all worldwide planned flights were canceled as of now. This is just the beginning.

62088619
Oil will keep going up at least until Trump gets on TV with tears on his face and begs Iran to open the strait. That's the bare minimum. He'll probably have to suck a donkey before this is all over with.

62088625
>>62087540
And made 20k in the last month thru leaps and selling shorts KEKAROOOO

62088778
"USOY" nigger what the fuck are you doing, buy futures like a functional human being

62088805
kek oil baggies

62091031
>>62088778
I don't have time to trade myself. I buy shit and sit on it for months if not a year. USOY basically does this >>62088625 on autopilot which is good if you expect the price to crawl up or even crab. Hell even a soft downward trend will print you money.

62091254
>>62087618
>Why isn't my paper oil valued as high as real oil?
Try and run your car on the barrels of oil you buy online and ask that question again.
Retard

62091310
>>62087540
i bought blackstone

62091385
>>62088619
Iran already bent the knee though

62091399
>>62091385
Nah Trump basically surrendered. This is the 10 point he agreed to.

62091409
>>62087540
So, I don’t understand anything about economics or anything. I’m literally retarded, but am I right holding my BTC and SOL? I didn’t let them shake me out.

62091494
>>62088605
Laugh at this retard

62091575
>>62088605
>There's no way oil doesn't reach $200 this year
Kek baggy. Better prepare the storage tanks in the basement for when they deliver it to you.

item.title: any WFH chads can you tell me how you found your job I have hospitality, customer service and even low level management… — 38 replies — 6.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
any WFH chads can you tell me how you found your job I have hospitality, customer service and even low level management…

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-04-08 06:57 UTC

OP

any WFH chads can you tell me how you found your job
I have hospitality, customer service and even low level management experience but im not getting any offers on my applications
Should I get some cyber security certs? which ones should I focus on if yes?

Early replies (first 3)

62084718
Funny of you to ask OP, I remember my struggles of landing my first WFH job. Btw, WFH stands for Work from Home because not many people come across that term usually. But, if you must ask, our company is headquartered in Nashville. My base pay is 95k per year along with a COL stipend and generous health and pension benefits. As we speak, I am clocked into my position in the beautiful hot springs in British Columbia. We do have an RTO policy, but I haven't been to the office in 4 years! Ha-Ha! You COULDN'T pay me to work in the office. Yesterday I had fun fucking a uni student that transferred from china for the school years, so yeah life is great so far. Today she is making me her traditional Chinese dinner as a thank you for fucking her so nicely. Maybe next month I'll fly out to Holland and do my job for the month. But like I said OP, keep your chin up and don't be disuaded by the multiple rejection emails in your future until you get that golden offer. Take it and run with it. Anyways .... Chiao!

62084729
>>62084718
thanks for the response anon
Did you use indeed or pay for a website that specializes in WFH jobs like flexjobs etc? I'm wondering if indeed is just all scams at this point and if it might be worth it to pay a job finding website to filter the garbage

62084746
There are no jobs anymore. 200,000 people have been laid off in the last six months while job creation is negative. 90% of job openings go to immigrants anyway.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62085002
>>62084678
so many green id’s in this thread
good sign op

62085269
>>62084791
best to give up and just hate on glowies by posting things they hate like Epstein
your inability to find a job is a sign to make their lives miserable or else they would make sure you found work

62085740
Demoralizers are right. Your best bet is to make a YouTube channel going over the Epstein files. That shit is huge now. Everyone here should be doing 4chan schizo shit on social media because everything we’ve been talking about, banker greed, Epstein abuse, conspiracy, ww3 apocalypse is now becoming mainstream. Most of you saw this coming. Time to capitalize on it

62085750
>>62085740
>Demoralizers are right about there being no more normie jobs

62085875
>>62084678
>Should I get some cyber security certs? which ones should I focus on if yes?
No, because literally every retard like you has decided that cybersecurity is a comfy do-nothing job and EVERYONE has flooded the industry. There were not enough cyber security jobs 10 years ago, there sure aren't enough today now that it has been meme'd as retard daycare.

62085899
>>62084678
Honestly data analytics is an easy WFH career path. Just learn SQL and either PBI, Tableau, or Qlik depending on what company you want to work for. Maybe some python would be good too, but I got a job a few years ago basically only knowing SQL and making a follow along tutorial Tableau dashboard.

62085904
>>62085875
Exactly this. OP should focus their attention on destroying glowfucks and not something that only neckbeard redditors want to do. Cybersecurity was always fake and for fags

62086811
>>62084759
>They hire new people every day faggot
yeah in hyderabad

62086826
>>62084746
Why you call them “immigrants” man? Those are not immigrants, those are pure turdies in turdworld
It’s called off sourcing

62087064
>>62084678
green id get

62087137
>>62084678
got it years ago

62087226
>>62085899
Thank you anon. Sounds like a plan, I am currently learning Python, is SQL hard to learn?
So basically I should look for jobs as Data Analyst, can I try to apply even without any experience in the field? I have been working as a development engineer since 7 years and looking to change careers into 100% WFH.

62087308
>>62087226
SQL is definitely easier than python. If you’re not a complete retard it shouldn’t take too long to become proficient. And yeah you can apply without any experience, data analyst is usually an entry level role. Just find some datasets online and make some dashboards that look nice and say you know SQL, that was enough for me to get a job. It’s not a bad career path because you can transition into data engineering pretty easily from data analyst and data engineers can make a lot of money at the right companies.

62087410
>>62084678
Why are you people still keeping the belief that wfh jobs are attainable alive? Its a complete scam, especially now.

>>62085904
>>62085875
This is right
>t. Fell for the cyber security meme because I thought I would be able to wfh

3 years later and I never got a job in the field let alone a wfh one. I fell for the scam. Don't do what I did.

62088425
>>62085875
I lucked into one of those roles and it sucked (apart from having ok pay and being WFH)
I'm sure people can succeed in that role but it's one of those roles where there's an autistic degree of variance in terms of responsibility and buy-in from the rest of the org, to most orgs it's just a cost center that gets in the way of productivity

62088526
>>62084678
I got laid off from my last job in March 2025. I was WFH, thanks to covid we all went WFH. 9 years at the company and genuinely the top guy. I was team lead, I did the training, I wrote the manuals, I had the highest numbers. I worked in fraud prevention for ecomm. I went to meetings with the big dogs while my manager who knew less about our programs stayed with the rest of the team. I loved my bosses. They loved me. I got laid off after training 10 Filipinos. So did the rest of our NA Fraud team and our manager. Replaced by the Flips. I job hunted with good references and great resume. I only got 2 interviews in 6 months, finally landed a shitty call center job. Had to take it because I needed work.

No one is hiring WFH unless you're something special or have certs others cant easily get. Why pay you 20+/hr when they can hire a flip for 2.50/hr. You can do more work? Okay, I can hire 4 flips and its still less than hiring you. If not flips, indians.

62088548
>>62084678
I'm going back to school for cyber security. What certs do you recommend? I have a security clearance which I hope gets me an entry level job at least

62088565
>>62084678
I was in my job for 8 years then Covid hit and we all just went home permanently. There was a 2 year period where we weren’t sure if we were going to come back to the office or not, but finally they said we were permanently remote.

62088593
>>62084678
I took an internship straight out of college (2018) which turned into a software dev job. Am senior dev now. We went fully remote during covid and have been ever since

62088599
>>62088526
This kind of disloyalty should be punishable by public hanging

62088666
>>62088599
yup I agree. my boss' hands were tied, he is still there, the flips are dogshit and theyve had to hire even more to try and cover all the work the American team was doing. They have a flip manager too. Not a single American on the entire ecomm fraud team. Bossman says its only a matter of time until the entire company is flips. I don't have anything against the flips, some of them were really cool. And it's not like they caused me to get laid off. Its the greedy Indian CEO who caused it. And Id much rather hear a Pilipino accent than an Indian accent if I ever had to call in to speak to customer service.

62090239
>>62088548
Grow a neck beard and get fat because it’s worked for every other cyber security worker

62090283
>>62086811
this and other anons are right. Stop focusing on getting hired and focus on making content designed to piss off glowies. That’s a market that is actually growing. AI content especially. Just look at all the popular YouTube channels. You either need to make ai slop or Candace Owens, Tucker Carlson, Jimmy Dore, Theo Von, Dave Smith type stuff. Anti-Israel content like greyzone, etc

Most people here are thinking working for the man is the answer but the right answer is destroying what the man has built

62090311
>>62088526
>No one is hiring WFH unless you're something special or have certs others cant easily get.
I’ve gotten like 5 or 6 wfh jobs between 2022-2026 just by lying on my resume. Getting offers is definitely harder than it was back then but not impossible. I think if anything I’m overqualified, hence why I’ve had so many interviews but not many offers recently, so I just took off one job and slightly extended the timeline on another one.

62090709
>>62088526
Imagine getting replace by the pagpag eater

62090732
>>62084678
Be indispensable at your job. Say you're going to move and can either work remote from home or find another job. Watch them find a way to let you work remote. Simple as.

62090748
Mine came naturally after years of working in the tech field. If you study Azure a.k.a Entra, Exchange Admin, Sharepoint Admin, InTune, ETC, you'll move from the nerd who tells retards how to reboot their computers to endpoint management or some kind of administrator. Most of those jobs can be done from anywhere. You just get a company laptop, connect to a VPN.

62090930
>>62084718
based

62091196
Got an interview coming up for a WFH job as a claims adjuster. Paid training and all. Starting off is 64k a year. Much better than my shitty county job. I hope I get it.

62091298
>>62091196
Good luck anon. I have a good feeling you’ll get it

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-04-08 11:37 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62091318
>>62091218

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62091324
>>62088615

>>62091218
>>62091318

Pretty sure the moderators of this website have proven that these kind of threads are unwarranted.

And now y'all continue to make them?

Fucking chainlink baggies are so mad about XRP's success they gotta keep forcing this shitty boom-meme.

62091692
There might be some schizo connection here. The Rothschilds basically own sri lanka and they often test out their devious world domination plans on the natives. Two years ago the sri lankan government banned the use of artificial fertilizer and the country's rice production dropped by 40%. Sounds like a test run to me. The Rothschilds are also one of Ripple's biggest shareholders.

62091732
>>62091324

dont you understand layers of irony?

BOOOOOOOOM SO BULLISH XRP
